revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun]][[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Ancient Kofuns in Ōgaki, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Hiruiōzuka Kofun | native_name = 昼飯大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Hirui Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hiruiōzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hiruiōzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.387519|N|136.571389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki, Gifu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Hiruiōzuka Kofun'''|昼飯大塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164461|title= 昼飯大塚古墳|trans-title= Hiruiōzuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Hiruiōzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumuli in Gifu Prefecture and dates from the mid-4th century. It is located on the Makino plateau at an elevation of 25 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure, orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of 150 meters. The bottom tier of the structure and its surrounding moat are now underground due to generations of agricultural activity in the area. The site is also being encroached upon by residential housing developments. It was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1980 by {{ill|Nagoya University|en|Nagoya University|qid=Q1191132|lt=Nagoya University|1=Nagoya University}} and was found to contain three separate [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one with a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} in the north, one with a clay sarcophagus in the south and one with a wooden coffin in the west. The stone sarcophagus had been looted in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and the clay sarcophagus was not excavated. The burial chamber with wooden coffin was found to contain many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including iron and steel swords, tools made from iron, along with jade balls, ''[[magatama]]'', glass ornaments and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery fragments. The inside of the coffin was not excavated. ''Haniwa'' recovered from the site was in various forms, including cylindrical-shaped ''haniwa'', and ''haniwa'' in the shape of houses, human figures and in the form of tools and shields. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The ''kofun'' has been restored to what archaeologists believe to be its original appearance, covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' with ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and the moat was also restored, forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. The site is a three-minute walk from the "Akasaka Sōgō Center" on the Meihan Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Ogaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|qid=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
* Total length: 150 meters * Posterior circular portion: 99 meters diameter x 12 meters high * Anterior rectangular portion: 80 meters wide x 9.5 meters high
==Gallery== <gallery> Hirui Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|top view 昼飯大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa 昼飯大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|iron swords </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|qid=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Hirui Otsuka Kofun}} *[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/0000000695.html Ogaki city home page] {{in lang|ja}}
